topic: Decision Making to Improve Financial Performance
subject: business-studies

items.q: Why can a profitable business still fail?
items.correct: Because cash flow and profit are different — a business may be profitable on paper but run out of cash to pay bills, especially if customers owe money or stock ties up funds.
items.misconception: If a business is profitable it cannot run out of money.
items.random_wrong: Profitable businesses fail only when employees steal.
items.why: Cash-flow problems sink profitable companies routinely. Profit is accounting; cash is liquidity. Students treat the two as equivalent and miss why businesses go bust despite paper profits.

items.correct: Competitive rivalry, threat of new entrants, threat of substitutes, bargaining power of suppliers, bargaining power of buyers — together determining industry attractiveness and potential profitability.
items.misconception: Porter's Five Forces are five business strategies a firm should choose between.
items.random_wrong: Porter's Five Forces are five physical security measures for a factory.
items.why: Five Forces analyse INDUSTRY structure, not firm strategy choices (that's Porter's generic strategies, a different framework). Students conflate the two Porter models.

items.q: What is the difference between gross profit and net profit?
items.correct: Gross profit = revenue minus cost of goods sold (direct costs only); net profit = revenue minus ALL costs including overheads, interest and tax. Net profit is the bottom line.
items.misconception: Gross profit and net profit are the same thing.
items.random_wrong: Gross profit is profit before VAT and net profit is profit after VAT.
items.why: The gap between gross and net reveals overhead burden. A high gross but low net margin means heavy fixed costs or financing. Students conflate the two and misread profitability.

items.q: Modigliani and Miller's irrelevance theorem was published in:
items.why: Correct answer: 1958. “1948” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
Correct answer: Cost of capital. “Annual cost calculation” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year did Modigliani and Miller publish their capital structure theorem?
items.why: Exact answer: 1958 AD. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
